expectations.text: Output file is named architecture-agentique.md (.md extension, not .txt)
Output uses Markdown headings/lists for the flowing text (title, explanatory bullets, 'TYPES DE RELATIONS' block)
expectations.evidence: 'TYPES DE RELATIONS' is left as flat caps text, no '#'/'##' heading markers added — plain verbatim dump per old skill design

expectations.text: The diagram section correctly states that Agent 1 (recherche générale) and Agent 2 (sources officielles) are launched in parallel by the Head Agent
expectations.evidence: Raw box dump includes 'PARALLÈLE' label immediately above 'AGENT 1 / Recherche Générale' and 'AGENT 2 / Recherche Officielles', so the words are present, though as a positional label rather than an explained relationship

expectations.text: The diagram section correctly states that Agent 3 runs sequentially after Agent 1 and Agent 2 both finish, and that it synthesizes/combines their results — it must not present Agent 3 as a second parallel agent or duplicate the Agent 1/Agent 2 pair in a confusing second block
expectations.evidence: The raw transcription shows 'PARALLÈLE / AGENT 3 / (... e -> ...)' BEFORE the second 'AGENT 1 / AGENT 2' repeat and the 'SÉQUENTIEL / AGENT 3 Synthèse' line — i.e. Agent 3 is first labeled under a 'PARALLÈLE' heading and only later, in a duplicated second block, associated with 'SÉQUENTIEL'. This is exactly the confusing duplicate/ambiguous presentation the assertion flags.

expectations.text: The Résumé section is consistent with the diagram section's flow (same parallel-then-sequential relationship), stated briefly
expectations.evidence: Summary text correctly describes parallel/séquentiel/hybride (fan-out puis pipeline) relations in prose, independent of the ambiguous raw diagram dump above it

eval_feedback.overall: 2/5 pass. The flat positional transcription of diagram box labels reproduces the same 'PARALLÈLE before Agent 3' ambiguity that caused the wrong reading in iteration 1 — the Résumé prose is fine on its own, but the Diagramme section it should be grounded in is structurally confusing.
